_doc: Owner CITED ERA-YEAR overrides for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.5).
WHAT THIS LAYER IS. sourceStartYear is the earliest senior-club membership START
per Wikidata's P54 statements (age >= 16, first teams). E2.1's blind re-verify
(drawCardsReal.verify2.json, committed) established that this is systematically
NOT the competitive debut: a signing / squad-registration / academy-intake /
reserve-season / friendly year sits where a debut was assumed on ~25% of the
cards for which a qualifying non-Wikidata source was actually retrieved.
The fact model was realigned rather than patched: no date is a published fact,
and sourceStartYear is published as membership-start provenance, not a debut. The
one place a year still DECIDES something the player sees is the ERA BUCKET
(eraYear -> peakYear := eraYear + 5 -> eraIndex). So this layer supplies eraYear.
RULE (Ticket E0.5 item 2). eraYear := citedValue where a record here exists, else
sourceStartYear. A record is minted ONLY for a verify2 RED whose contradiction
CROSSES an era bucket under the committed mapping — i.e. where the wrong year
would file the player in the wrong era. A within-bucket contradiction changes no
bucket and is recorded as ACCEPTED NOISE (see acceptedNoise below), NOT overridden:
minting a record that moves nothing would be a decision pretending to be one, the
same anti-pattern the debut/nation rulings guard against.
PROVENANCE. Every citedValue is taken from the verifier's OWN citation — publisher,
resolvable URL and verbatim quote are reproduced here, unaltered. This layer never
edits sourceStartYear: the sourced value stands beside the override in the dossier,
so a reader always sees what the source said and that a human overruled the ERA on
a named, quoted citation. The value the card renders (eraIndex/eraLabel) is the
only thing that changes.
KEY IS qid, NOT cardId. cardId is real_<fameRank> and fameRank is positional, so
it SHIFTS whenever the pool changes (E0.5 drops van der Sar and De Bruyne, moving
every lower rank up). Overrides are applied to the POOL before selection re-runs,
exactly like the position/nation/debut rulings, so they must key on the stable qid.
The cardId field below is the id AT THE TIME verify2 was written — an audit pointer
into that file's `red` array, expected to drift after the rebuild, never the key.
NOT HERE, ON PURPOSE:
- Falcao (real_0205, Q206567): verify2 flags 1999 (Guinness/Bogota Post), which
IS bucket-crossing, but the 1999 'debut' was at age 13 and is excluded by the
pipeline's own age-16 rule. Under the realigned model there is no contradiction
left: sourceStartYear is the earliest SENIOR start, and a 13-year-old's match is
not senior. Recorded as a definitional non-defect, flagged for owner, NOT
overridden. Overriding it would file a 2010s icon into the 2000s against the
pipeline's own definition.
- Every within-bucket RED (Owen, Rashford, Cavani, Giroud, Ramsey, Gomez,
Szczesny, Bonucci, Gattuso, Romero, Otamendi, Banks, Sommer, Benatia-date): the
cited correction is real but changes no era bucket, so it is accepted noise. Note
Gordon Banks (real_0337): his 5-year error (1953 youth contract vs 1958 debut) is
the headline signing-vs-debut case, yet BOTH years sit in bucket 0 (peak <= 1980),
so no override is minted. The ticket's 'Banks class' names the contradiction
class, not a bucket crossing; Banks himself crosses nothing.

acceptedNoise.name: Ricardo Carvalho
acceptedNoise.note: 1996 disproven (debut at 20 => 1998/99) but no citation pins a replacement year; 1996 and 1998/99 both fall in bucket 2, so era is unaffected regardless.

records.name: Karim Benzema
records.note: First-team debut 15 Jan 2005 v Metz. Claimed 2004 traces to a reserve-team promotion / the 2004-05 season label; in autumn 2004 he played only Lyon's CFA fourth-division reserve side.

records.name: Sergio Aguero
records.quote: On 5 July 2003, Sergio Aguero (Argentina, b. 2 June 1988) made his Primera Division debut for Independiente aged 15 years 33 days.
records.note: Sourced 2006 is his Atletico Madrid arrival. Live Wikidata carries Independiente 2003-2006; the age-16 clip rewrote the start to 2004, erasing the real 2003 debut, and the surviving surfaced value landed on the later transfer.

records.name: Keisuke Honda
records.quote: In 2005, his first professional season, he made his debut as a starter since the opening day
records.note: Nagoya membership surfaced from 2004; the league's own record puts the first professional season at 2005.

records.name: Arda Turan
records.quote: Arda Turan, Galatasaray'daki ilk profesyonel macina 22 Ocak 2005 gunu Izmit Ismetpasa Stadi'nda Bursaspor'a karsi cikti.
records.note: Club's own site dates the first professional match to 22 January 2005.

records.name: David Trezeguet
records.quote: Il connait son premier match en professionnel en Ligue 1 en fevrier 1996
records.note: Club's own site dates the first professional Ligue 1 match to February 1996; 1994 is a squad-registration year.

records.name: John Obi Mikel
records.note: Lyn's senior record is Tippeligaen 2005 only (6 apps); there is no 2004 season row. Archive-tier source per the CIE policy.

records.name: Joao Moutinho
records.quote: Sporting-Pampilhosa de 4 de janeiro de 2005, jogo da Taca de Portugal, marcou a estreia de Joao Moutinho como senior.
records.note: B-team class: his 2003/04 season was Sporting B (a reserve side, excluded). First-team senior debut 4 Jan 2005.

records.name: David Ospina
records.quote: Debuto en 2006 y por dos anos consecutivos el hincha verde durmio tranquilo.
records.note: Club's own site dates the debut to 2006, corroborated by Pulzo pinning the first league match to 5 Feb 2006 v Cucuta. 2004 unsupported by any source found.
